resolution: Only the public dry-run path can produce a plan accepted by downstream formal boundaries; the ad hoc regeneration script was removed.
A schema-validated formal scope input now carries the exact Phase 1 task and policy contract.
Toolchain Provenance Binding now detects ordinary plan/receipt tampering and binds target/framework Git state, but it is intentionally not described as keyed cryptographic anti-forgery.
The compiler now generates canonical write-set, pre-install hashes, and manual rollback manifests before target writes.
Existing AGENTS/config/scripts assets are deterministically classified as manual adoption assets and are never auto-overwritten; automatic semantic reconciliation remains out of scope.
Malformed legacy merge candidates remain superseded. This minimum unblock does not add an automatic AGENTS merge engine.
Active development requires non-empty allowed paths at every boundary; empty scope is accepted only for explicitly Owner-confirmed observation mode.
The remediation report is the authoritative current index and labels prior Phase 0.5 acceptance artifacts as superseded evidence only.
Formal outputs use UTF-8/LF writers and C0 scans. The three C0 bytes remain preserved only in the legacy Replay evidence rollup and are not copied into current handoff artifacts.
